summary: On the night of June 2-3, 2026, Russia launched 198 strike drones (Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and decoy types) against Ukraine from multiple directions; Ukrainian air defenses shot down or suppressed 189 of them, with 8 drones hitting 7 locations. A Ukrainian drone struck a Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus in Yenakiieve, DNR, killing up to 8 people, while Ukrainian drones also attacked infrastructure in Kronstadt, Kirovsky, and Krasnoselsky districts of St. Petersburg, and an oil terminal in St. Petersburg caught fire. Russian forces continued active pressure on Kostiantynivka, advancing infiltration south of Illinka and toward Novodmytrivka, while Ukrainian drones struck the corvette Boyky in Kronstadt port and Russian forces used Geranium drones to destroy a truck carrying a UAV launcher.
key_points: Russia launched 198 strike drones at Ukraine overnight; 189 were shot down, 8 hit 7 locations across north, south, and east. (Source: @kpszsu/63744)
Ukrainian drone hit Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus in Yenakiieve, DNR, killing up to 8 people. (Source: @tass_agency/378861)
Ukrainian drones attacked infrastructure in Kronstadt, Kirovsky, and Krasnoselsky districts of St. Petersburg; several injured. (Source: @tass_agency/378871)
An oil terminal in St. Petersburg's Coal Harbour caught fire after a Ukrainian drone attack. (Source: @UkraineNow/81638)
Ukrainian forces struck the corvette Boyky in Kronstadt port. (Source: @eRadarrua/95170)
Russian forces intensified pressure on Kostiantynivka, advancing infiltration south of Illinka and toward Novodmytrivka. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)
Russian Geranium drone destroyed a truck carrying a Ukrainian UAV launcher in the SMO zone. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30650)
Zelensky warned of a possible massive attack on the night of June 3. (Source: @UkraineNow/81634)

sections.executive_summary: The most operationally significant development is Russia's massive overnight drone assault on June 2-3, 2026, launching 198 strike drones from multiple directions including Bryansk, Kursk, Millerovo, Orel, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, and Guardiyske in Crimea. Ukrainian air defenses shot down or suppressed 189 drones, but 8 struck 7 locations across the north, south, and east of the country, with debris falling at 7 additional locations. Simultaneously, Ukrainian drones struck strategic Russian targets including the corvette Boyky in Kronstadt port, infrastructure in St. Petersburg's Kronstadt, Kirovsky, and Krasnoselsky districts, and an oil terminal in St. Petersburg's Coal Harbour, while a Ukrainian drone hit a Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus in Yenakiieve, DNR, killing up to 8 people.

points: Ukrainian air defense engaged 198 enemy strike drones (Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and decoy 'Parody' types) launched from Bryansk, Kursk, Millerovo, Orel, Primorsko-Akhtarsk in Russia, and Guardiyske in temporarily occupied Crimea during the night of June 2-3. Aviation, anti-aircraft missile troops, EW units, drone units, and mobile fire groups participated in the defense. 189 drones were shot down or suppressed across the north, south, and east, with 8 drones hitting 7 locations and debris falling at 7 additional locations. (Source: @kpszsu/63744)

Ukrainian drones attacked Russian infrastructure in Kronstadt, Kirovsky, and Krasnoselsky districts of St. Petersburg, with several people injured according to St. Petersburg authorities. An oil terminal in St. Petersburg's Coal Harbour caught fire after a Ukrainian drone strike; this is described as one of the key Russian terminals for transshipment of petroleum products in the Baltic region. (Source: @tass_agency/378871, @UkraineNow/81638)
Ukrainian forces struck the corvette Boyky in Kronstadt port, according to the Forces of Unmanned Systems. (Source: @eRadarrua/95170)

A Ukrainian drone struck a Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus in Yenakiieve, DNR, early on June 3, killing 7 people initially reported, with the death toll later rising to 8 according to Pushilin. (Source: @tass_agency/378865, @tass_agency/378861)

Ukrainian drones were active across multiple directions: Dnipropetrovsk region (Vasylkivka, Shakhtarske, Solone, Hirnytske), Zaporizhzhia region, Chernihiv region (toward Slavutych, Ichnia, between Nizhyn and Borzna), Mykolaiv, Odesa region (Tatarbunary direction from the Black Sea), Rivne region, and toward Dnipro and Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @kpszsu/63764, @kpszsu/63745, @kpszsu/63737, @kpszsu/63743, @kpszsu/63754, @kpszsu/63772, @eRadarrua/95189)
Zelensky warned that intelligence indicated a massive enemy attack could occur on the night of June 3, urging citizens to pay attention to air raid alerts. (Source: @UkraineNow/81634)

In the Mykolaiv region, women armed with shovels drove away military commissars. Ukrainian Ombudsman Lubinets criticized the TCC reform, stating that the use of force, arrests, and confiscation of personal belongings by people in balaclavas continues despite assurances this would stop. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30647, @ukr_leaks_eng/30624)

Russia launched a massive drone attack on Ukraine overnight June 2-3, deploying 198 strike drones of types Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and decoy 'Parody' drones from multiple launch directions: Bryansk, Kursk, Millerovo, Orel, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, and Guardiyske in Crimea. (Source: @kpszsu/63744)

In the Leningrad region, at least 30 Ukrainian drones were shot down; the enemy spread footage of fires at an oil terminal in the Coal Harbour of St. Petersburg. Moscow Mayor Sobyanin reported 22 drones flying toward the capital were downed. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

In the Tambov region, a multi-story building, a library, and an art school were damaged with windows broken, as well as outbuildings of an industrial enterprise. The target also included the Progress plant in Mikhailovsk. In the Rostov region, drones were destroyed in the Millerovo district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

In the Bryansk region, a 6-year-old girl was injured when drone debris fell into a multi-story building courtyard. A drone fell onto a shopping center parking lot in Bryansk city with no injuries. Ukrainian forces fired from multiple rocket launchers at the village of Suzemka, killing a civilian. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

In the Kursk region, a local resident was injured in the village of Maloe Soldatskoe in the Belovsky district due to a drone attack. Two men were injured in Belaya, Belovsky district. A civilian was injured when a drone attacked a car in the village of Vysheye Derevnyki, Lgovsky district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

In the Belgorod region, a Ukrainian drone detonated on a commercial facility in Shebekino, injuring a civilian. An FPV drone attacked a bus in the same area. Near Posokhovo in the Valuysky district, an FPV drone attacked a moving car, injuring two men. In Bochkovka, Belgorod district, a drone attacked a private house, injuring an 11-year-old boy with multiple shrapnel wounds to his legs. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

The Russian Armed Forces detected and destroyed a truck carrying a Ukrainian UAV launcher in the SMO zone using a Geranium kamikaze drone. Russian aerial bombs and missiles are destroying temporary Ukrainian Armed Forces deployment sites in the Kharkov and Zaporozhye regions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30650, @ukr_leaks_eng/30633)

On the Sumy front, in the Shostka district, assault troops of the 'North' Group are engaged in infantry battles in Bachevsk. In the Sumy district, assault troops advanced up to 700 meters in 11 sectors, with battles ongoing in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka, and the vicinity of populated areas. In the Krasnopol district, battles are taking place between Lesnoye and Taratutino, and northwest of Novodmytrovka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

On the Kharkov front, units of the 'North' Group are advancing toward Kachya Lopan', described as a major logistics center of the Armed Forces of Ukraine. Infantry battles are taking place in Shevchenko and near Na Volchansk. On the Volchansk sector, units advanced up to 500 meters in eight sectors, with infantry battles in Okhrymovka and forested areas of the Volchansk district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)
In the south of the Kupyansk direction, reports indicate an advance in the Kupyansk-Uzlovoe settlement. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

In the DPR, a taxi driver was killed on the ring road in the Leninsky district of Donetsk. A man was injured on the Donetsk-Gorlovka highway. Peaceful residents were injured in Uglegorsk, Svetlodarsk, Gorlovka, and Selidovo. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

Russian forces continue active pressure on Kostiantynivka, with the red zone expanding south of Illinka where the enemy is pushing to increase control of terrain around the city. The infiltration zone into the city is also growing. The enemy is also active on the eastern side of the city in the area of Stupochki and Predtechnoye, attempting to occupy Novodmytrivka for infiltration purposes. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

The occupation of Illinka and Novodmytrivka threatens the northern part of Kostiantynivka, where the enemy aims to cut off access to other parts of the city. The enemy is building up advantage on the Kostiantynivka sector, reinforced by continuous KAB strikes on the city. Defending units reportedly lack sufficient personnel to hold the pressure. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

In the Dnipropetrovsk region, enemy strike drones were detected near Vasylkivka and Shakhtarske heading west, and near Solone and Hirnytske. Drones were also reported heading toward Dnipro (Yavornytske and Synelnykove) and Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @kpszsu/63764, @kpszsu/63745, @kpszsu/63737, @eRadarrua/95220, @kpszsu/63772)

In the Chernihiv region, a group of enemy drones was detected heading toward Slavutych. Additional drones were reported heading toward Ichnia and between Nizhyn and Borzna. Enemy drones were also detected on the border of Chernihiv region and Belarus heading south. (Source: @kpszsu/63746, @eRadarrua/95189, @kpszsu/63765)
Enemy tactical aviation was active on the northern and southeastern directions. (Source: @kpszsu/63767)
A threat of ballistic weapons use was reported from the northeast direction. (Source: @kpszsu/63717)
In the Leningrad region, at least 30 Ukrainian drones were shot down. Moscow Mayor Sobyanin reported 22 drones flying toward the capital were downed. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

In the Bryansk region, a 6-year-old girl was injured by drone debris in a multi-story building courtyard. A drone fell onto a shopping center parking lot in Bryansk city. Ukrainian MRL fire hit Suzemka, killing a civilian. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)
In the Kursk region, injuries were reported in Maloe Soldatskoe (Belovsky district), Belaya (two men), and Vysheye Derevnyki (Lgovsky district, civilian car attack). (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

In the Belgorod region, Ukrainian drones struck a commercial facility in Shebekino, a bus in the same area, a car near Posokhovo (Valuysky district, two men injured), and a private house in Bochkovka (11-year-old boy injured with shrapnel wounds to legs). (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

In the Zaporizhzhia region, a strike drone was reported in the 1st group area. Drones were also reported heading toward Zaporizhzhia from Dnipropetrovsk region. (Source: @eRadarrua/95198, @kpszsu/63772)
In the Mykolaiv region, enemy drones were reported over the city, and residents were urged to take shelter. (Source: @kpszsu/63742)

In the Odesa region, drones were detected from the Black Sea heading toward Tatarbunary. A drone was also reported heading toward Chornomorsk from the Black Sea. (Source: @kpszsu/63743, @kpszsu/63757)

In the DPR, a Ukrainian drone struck a Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus in Yenakiieve, killing up to 8 people. A taxi driver was killed on the ring road in Donetsk's Leninsky district. A man was injured on the Donetsk-Gorlovka highway. Civilians were injured in Uglegorsk, Svetlodarsk, Gorlovka, and Selidovo. (Source: @tass_agency/378861, @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)
In the Dnipropetrovsk region, strike drones were reported near Vasylkivka and Shakhtarske heading west, and near Solone and Hirnytske. (Source: @kpszsu/63764, @kpszsu/63745, @kpszsu/63737)
In Crimea, combat operations were ongoing overnight. The previous evening, footage was spread of a passenger train being hit and damaged in the north of Crimea. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)
In the Rostov region, drones were destroyed in the Millerovo district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

Russia launched 198 strike drones (Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and decoy 'Parody' types) against Ukraine overnight June 2-3 from Bryansk, Kursk, Millerovo, Orel, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, and Guardiyske in Crimea. Ukrainian air defenses shot down or suppressed 189 drones across the north, south, and east, with 8 drones hitting 7 locations and debris at 7 additional locations. (Source: @kpszsu/63744)

Ukrainian drones attacked infrastructure in Kronstadt, Kirovsky, and Krasnoselsky districts of St. Petersburg, injuring several people. An oil terminal in St. Petersburg's Coal Harbour caught fire. The corvette Boyky in Kronstadt port was struck. (Source: @tass_agency/378871, @UkraineNow/81638, @eRadarrua/95170)
In the Leningrad region, at least 30 Ukrainian drones were shot down. Moscow Mayor Sobyanin reported 22 drones flying toward the capital were downed. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634)

Enemy tactical aviation was active on the northern and southeastern directions. Threats of ballistic weapons use were reported from the northeast and from the east. (Source: @kpszsu/63767, @kpszsu/63717, @kpszsu/63773)

Ukrainian drones were detected across multiple axes: Chernihiv region (toward Slavutych, Ichnia, between Nizhyn and Borzna), Dnipropetrovsk region (Vasylkivka, Shakhtarske, Solone, Hirnytske), toward Dnipro (Yavornytske, Synelnykove), Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, Odesa region (Tatarbunary from Black Sea), Rivne region, and toward Chornomorsk from the Black Sea. (Source: @kpszsu/63746, @eRadarrua/95189, @kpszsu/63764, @kpszsu/63745, @kpszsu/63737, @eRadarrua/95220, @kpszsu/63772, @kpszsu/63742, @kpszsu/63743, @kpszsu/63754, @kpszsu/63757)
A Russian Geranium kamikaze drone destroyed a truck carrying a Ukrainian UAV launcher in the SMO zone. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30650)

Russian aerial bombs and missiles are destroying temporary Ukrainian Armed Forces deployment sites in the Kharkov and Zaporozhye regions. Continuous KAB strikes are being used against Kostiantynivka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30633, @DeepStateUA/23547)

key_developments: Russia launched 198 strike drones (Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, decoy types) against Ukraine overnight June 2-3 from Bryansk, Kursk, Millerovo, Orel, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, and Guardiyske in Crimea; Ukrainian air defense shot down 189, with 8 hitting 7 locations. (Source: @kpszsu/63744)
A Ukrainian drone struck a Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus in Yenakiieve, DNR, on June 3, killing up to 8 people according to Pushilin. (Source: @tass_agency/378861)

Ukrainian drones attacked infrastructure in Kronstadt, Kirovsky, and Krasnoselsky districts of St. Petersburg, injuring several people, and an oil terminal in St. Petersburg's Coal Harbour caught fire. (Source: @tass_agency/378871, @UkraineNow/81638)
Ukrainian forces struck the corvette Boyky in Kronstadt port. (Source: @eRadarrua/95170)

Russian forces intensified pressure on Kostiantynivka, expanding the red zone south of Illinka and attempting to occupy Novodmytrivka for infiltration, threatening the northern part of the city. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)
A Russian Geranium kamikaze drone destroyed a truck carrying a Ukrainian UAV launcher in the SMO zone. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30650)
Zelensky warned that intelligence indicated a massive enemy attack could occur on the night of June 3, urging citizens to heed air raid alerts. (Source: @UkraineNow/81634)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo is sharply escalating on both sides. Russia's deployment of 198 drones in a single night represents a significant massed UAV assault, though the 95% interception rate suggests Ukrainian air defense remains effective. Ukraine's strikes on strategic targets in St. Petersburg — including the Kronstadt naval base, the Boyky corvette, and a major oil terminal — demonstrate an ability to project force deep into Russian territory during a high-profile economic forum. The situation around Kostiantynivka is deteriorating for Ukrainian defenders, with Russian forces advancing infiltration from multiple directions and defending units reportedly understrength. The threat of additional ballistic weapon use from the northeast and east remains active.
sections.regional_response: NATO Secretary General Rutte arrived in Kyiv, signaling continued high-level alliance engagement. Italian Defense Minister Guido Crosetto stated that Ukraine's EU accession is 'very difficult' from both political and economic perspectives, citing agricultural competition concerns that even Germany cannot tolerate. Hungarian Prime Minister Magyar announced readiness to meet Zelensky early the following week, conditional on reaching agreement on the status of the Hungarian language in Ukraine. The International Fencing Federation lifted sanctions against Russian and Belarusian athletes, allowing them to compete under their own flags starting with the 2026 World Senior Championships in Hong Kong.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian air defense performance was notable, neutralizing 189 of 198 incoming drones, though 8 successful strikes across 7 locations indicate saturation tactics are achieving some penetration. The Kostiantynivka sector reveals a critical manpower shortage, with reports that the Commander-in-Chief reduced infantry replenishment to 50 persons per month per brigade under a 'fair distribution' policy, while some units lost half their personnel to AWOL due to command issues. Russian forces are employing combined arms pressure on Kostiantynivka with continuous KAB strikes and infantry infiltration from multiple axes. The attack on the Moscow-Simferopol bus in Yenakiieve raises questions about target selection and civilian impact in the DNR.
